Now, in this case, after SAP confirmed the licenses are in place, I couldn t find the permission for Data Factory in either Analytics permissions - Role Maintenance or RBP (manage WFA switches ON).

NOTE: I have never marked that checkbox on my own, I always assumed it s automatic even though it is not grayed out like the other options below it.

Then I got the Data Factory to appear in the instance, went on with importing the template and immediately got the following error:

Strange, but then I went to check validation and found:

 

Still strange, since it s just the template and never had issues with it, went to the dimensions page - editing the dimension gives this error:

Also, trying to edit the SQL, clicking validate it also throws an error...it s the standard generic code and should work but well...

So I decided to just delete the 2 dimensions with the error and run the cube again.

After deleting them and getting back to Data Factory:

I have closed everything down and tried again back from importing a fresh template, all steps went exactly the same, so I am stuck.

Would you please be so kind to have a look into this?

Company ID: S001955610T1

Many thanks in advance!

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Step By Step Guide to Transport Table Contents from One Environment to Other in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Unless I misunderstood what should be the implementation of the ABAP program, method 3 described is not copying the table content from one environment to another, but instead copying content between two tables (same environment).

Thanks for your contribution though.

In addition to the methods you described, it is also possible to copy table entries via R3trans at OS level.
To do that, two control files have to be created (export_<table>.ctl and import_<table>.ctl) with respective content/format:
------------------------
export
client=<source_client>
file = ‘/usr/sap/trans/data/<TABLE_NAME>.dat’
select * from <TABLE>
------------------------

------------------------
import
file = ‘/usr/sap/trans/data/<TABLE>.dat’
client=<target_client>
------------------------

Then run R3trans:
For export: R3trans -w export_<table>.log export_<table>.ctl
And import: R3trans -w import_<table>.log import_<table>.ctl

entry.title: Re: Higher priority work order reservation should not be...
entry.content:
Hello @preethialevarthi ,

In such situation you can do an enhancement in work order. If the priority is low and user wants to block the material specifically for the order then at the time of saving the order the material will automatically transferred to a virtual storage location (HARD). Now this storage location will be copied in ur order under component tab. The same storage location will populate automatically at the time of goods issue. The other order will not be able to issue it because it has the normal storage location in order.

So no need to put any HARD indicator on reservation. Transfer your material to HARD location where others cannot issue it. In the maintenance order component tab the storage location should be grey and at the time of MIGO the storage location cannot be selected as HARD manually. Otherwise person with storage location as normal can manually change the storage location and try to get material from HARD storage location.

Hope that it will help.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Context Attributes for SAP Released Event Types
entry.content:
Hi @SafaBahoosh ,

thanks for this detailed article!

When trying to apply this, a few questions come to my mind.

Firstly, the example you describe about the sales order seems not to apply to my system (S4Hana onprem 2023 as you mentioned).
For example, the abstract entity D_SalesOrderCreated is missing the "allowExtension" metadata tag in my case. Is there anything that needs to be done/enabled in advance?

More importantly though, I noticed that several central business objects (i.e. Product, BusinessPartner, ProductionOrder) seem to not be suitable to the approach you are describing.
On the example of product. Business Accelerator Hub refers to I_PRODUCTTP_2. Not only is this behavior definition not extensible also there no event definitions inside. Using the relation explorer in eclipse, one can find R_PRODUCTTP (not extensible) which again refers to D_ProductCreates (not extensible) as event.

In summary this seems to me that there is no way to create outbound filters for Product at all. Can you elaborate if this is the case or what I am missing?

entry.content:
Dear all

i'm trying to directly use @SAP/xsodata for SAP Business ONE SCHEMA browsing.

I've copied the examples in the docs directory but, when i'm trying to consume the service, i got a weird error:

IS_COLUMN_VIEW is not defined.

Investigating further seems that you must change line 483 in metadataReader.js to 

let schema = entityType.schema || context.defaultSchema, sql = 'select IS_COLUMN_TABLE as "IS_COLUMN_TYPE" from SYS.TABLES where SCHEMA_NAME = ? AND TABLE_NAME = ? ' + 'union select IS_COLUMN_VIEW as "IS_COLUMN_TYPE" from SYS.VIEWS where SCHEMA_NAME = ? AND VIEW_NAME = ?', tableName = entityType.tableName, parameters = [schema, tableName, schema, tableName];​I.E. you MUST add SYS. to TABLES and VIEWS internal tables.

Is that a bug, it's by design or am i missing something?

 

Thanks in advance

Nicola

entry.title: Re: Cannot Install Crystal reports 2016 on Windows 11
entry.content:
Given the steps you've taken, here are some additional suggestions to help resolve the installation issue:


# Potential Solutions:

1. *Compatibility Mode*: Right-click the setup.exe file, select Properties, and under the Compatibility tab, check "Run this program in compatibility mode for" and select Windows 8 or Windows 7.
2. *Disable Windows Defender*: Temporarily disable Windows Defender to ensure it's not interfering with the installation.
3. *Install in a different location*: Try installing Crystal Reports 2016 in a different location, such as C:\CR2016, instead of the default Program Files (x86) directory.
4. *Check for conflicting applications*: Ensure that no other applications are running that might be conflicting with the installation, such as other SAP or BusinessObjects applications.
5. *Install the latest Windows updates*: Ensure your Windows 11 laptop is up-to-date with the latest patches and updates.
6. *Try a clean boot*: Perform a clean boot to start Windows with a minimal set of drivers and startup programs.
7. *Check the Event Viewer*: Review the Event Viewer logs for any errors related to the installation.
8. *Contact SAP Support*: Reach out to SAP Support for further assistance, as they may be able to provide more specific guidance or troubleshooting steps.

# Additional Questions:

1. Have you tried installing Crystal Reports 2016 on another Windows 11 machine to rule out any laptop-specific issues?
2. Are there any specific error messages or codes that appear during the installation process?
3. Have you checked the system requirements for Crystal Reports 2016 to ensure your laptop meets the minimum requirements?

By trying these additional steps and providing more information, we can work together to resolve the installation issue.

entry.title: Streamlining Warehouse Operations with Unified Delivery Note Templates in SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Using SAP Business Network, you can streamline warehouse operations by providing suppliers with a unified delivery note template to attach to shipped goods.

 

Suppliers can access the template while creating or viewing an advance shipping notice, which later becomes an inbound delivery in SAP. Here's how it appears to suppliers:

Templates can range from simple formats like this:

To more detailed, line-item-oriented designs:

Templates can also use QR codes instead of barcodes, provided your scanning system supports it. QR codes add an extra layer of security.

To implement this feature, SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration licenses include it in the deployment plan. The SAP Deployment team will handle the setup, starting with a requirements gathering session involving SAP Buyer integrator. After the template is built, basic testing ensures it meets your needs. Once finalized, templates are managed at the supplier group level.

The way you assign templates to suppliers is the same for test and production. Often customers would like to default a single template to all suppliers moving forward in production—that is possible in production. Unfortunately, you need to keep adding new suppliers as members of the group as part of your business-as-usual operations.

 

Notes from Past Project Experience:

  • Templates can also be configured for SBN Commerce Automation projects, though suppliers not involved in manufacturing are typically excluded.
  • In rare cases, templates have been used for standard account suppliers, though this can be challenging.

entry.title: Re: S/4 public cloud RFC call
entry.content:
Hi Marty,

ony usecase that I just came around is the maybe missing support for Variant Configuration in the OData API. Only the new OData v4 Sales Order API supports Variant Configuration. If you need Variant Configuration also for Inquiries or Sales Quotations then the only option is the BAPI_INQUIRY_CREATEFROMDATA2 or BAPI_QUOTATION_CREATEFROMDATA2.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Fundamental concepts for any SAP FICO consultant
entry.content:
Organizational Structure:

✔ Client: The highest level in the SAP system. It represents an independent organizational unit (e.g., a corporation). All other organizational units are assigned to a client.

✔Company Code: The central organizational unit in Financial Accounting (FI). It's an independent legal entity for which complete financial statements can be created (balance sheet, income statement).

✔Controlling Area: The central organizational unit in Controlling (CO). It's used for cost accounting and internal reporting. One or more company codes can be assigned to a controlling area. This is where cost and revenue flow.

✔Business Area: An organizational unit used for internal reporting based on different lines of business or geographical locations within a company code. It's less commonly used in modern SAP implementations, often replaced by profit centers or segments.

Financial Accounting (FI):

🌡 Chart of Accounts (COA): A list of all general ledger (G/L) accounts used by one or more company codes. It defines the structure and numbering of G/L accounts.

🌡 Account Groups: Control the fields that are required, optional, suppressed, or displayed when creating or changing G/L master records. They are defined at the chart of accounts level.

🌡 Fiscal Year Variant: Defines the start and end of the fiscal year and the number of posting periods. It's assigned to the company code.

🌡 Document Types: Classify accounting documents (e.g., invoice, payment, journal entry). They control the number range for documents and which account types can be posted to.

🌡 Posting Keys: Two-digit keys that determine the account type (e.g., G/L account, customer, vendor, asset), the debit/credit indicator, and other posting-relevant information.

🌡 Number Ranges: Define the numbering system for various objects, such as accounting documents, assets, and materials. They prevent duplicate numbers.

🌡 House Banks: The banks with which a company has accounts. Bank master data is maintained here, including account numbers, bank keys, and other relevant information for payment processing.

🌡 Ledger: In S4HANA, ledgers are the central repository for accounting data. The leading ledger (usually 0L) is used for legal reporting, while other ledgers (non-leading ledgers) can be used for parallel accounting according to different accounting principles (e.g., IFRS, US GAAP).

Asset Accounting (AA):

🧯Chart of Depreciation: A list of all depreciation areas used by one or more company codes. It defines the depreciation methods and parameters.

🧯Asset Class: A grouping of assets with similar characteristics (e.g., buildings, machinery, vehicles). It determines the default account assignments, depreciation keys, and other asset-related information.

🧯 Depreciation Area: Represents different valuation methods for assets (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation). Each asset can have multiple depreciation areas.

Materials Management (MM) / Controlling (CO) Integration:

✋Valuation Class: Links materials to G/L accounts in FI. It determines which accounts are debited or credited during goods movements.

✋ Material Ledger: In S4HANA, the Material Ledger is mandatory. It collects actual costs for materials and allows for different inventory valuation methods (e.g., moving average price, standard price). It is crucial for parallel valuation and actual costing.


Key Relationships:

Company codes are assigned to a client and a controlling area.

A chart of accounts is assigned to company codes.

A fiscal year variant is assigned to company codes.

A chart of depreciation is assigned to company codes.

Valuation classes link materials in MM to G/L accounts in FI (via account determination).

Understanding these relationships is crucial for configuring and troubleshooting SAP FICO. They represent the foundation of how financial and management accounting data is organized and processed within the system.

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_sap-activity-7281767669370949632-Wln4?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Re: Not able to upload values of UDF via DTW SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi,

To add UDFs to the Excel templates in DTW, follow these steps:

1- Login to DTW.
2- Go to Templates and select Customize Template.
3- Click on Select Object.
4- In your case, navigate to Inventory > Inventory Transactions > Goods Receipt.
5- Highlight Document and expand the drop-down list by clicking the + symbol to check if the UDFs you created appear in the list.
6- Once confirmed, right-click on Document and select Create Template for Structure.
This will generate a new template with the UDFs included, allowing you to populate and upload the required data.

Best regards,
Si mohamed ANDDAM

entry.title: Re: Step By Step Guide to Transport Table Contents from One Environment to Other in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Download and Upload High Volume

  • Use Case: This method is suitable for transferring large amounts of data 
  • Steps:
    1. Download Data:
      • Read data from the source table using an ABAP program.
      • Export the data to a file (e.g., CSV, XML).
    2. Upload Data:
      • Read the data from the file.
      • Import the data into the target table using an ABAP program.
  • Considerations:
    • This method is suitable for high-volume data transfers.
    • Data formatting and conversion might be required during the import and export process.
    • Ensure data integrity and consistency during the transfer.
    • Consider using appropriate data handling techniques (e.g., batch processing) to avoid performance issues.

If you need to copy table content between two different SAP systems, you could also use different methods, such as:

  • SLD (System Landscape Directory): This tool can be used to transport data between different systems in an SAP landscape.
  • File-based transfer: Export data from the source system to a file, and then import it into the target system. This method requires careful data formatting and handling.
  • Third-party tools: Several third-party tools are available for data migration between SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Remote table not reflecting measures properly in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

In your CDS view you have to join with the TCURX and alter the value accordingly. 

These currencies have only 1 decimal place, instead of 3. This causes issues in change logs, etc. for example. You have to catch this in the CDS, or create a DB view and then redirect your CDS to that DB view. 

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: Re: How to implement additional booking periods (More tha...
entry.content: Achso. Then create your own association on top of the time-tables. Based on CSV f.e. Or add the periods 13-16 via union all. And associate this new view to your model-view. This way you will have two different time-associations in the system, but the second will be for the periods 13-16. Another option is fiscal calendar, which contains these special periods used in CO.

entry.title: Light and Dark theme in SAP Analytics cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Objective: Implement theme switching in SAP Analytics Cloud using an optimized approach.

Solution: It is possible to implement by using Optimised Design Experience (ODE).

Dashboard

 These are the steps to achieve switching themes in SAP SAC.

  1. Script variable
  2. Theme preferences
  3. Switch implement and onChange script
  4. Story onInitalization script 

 

Step 1: 

  • Create a script variable and set "light" as the default value

Step 2:

  • Create two theme preferences, one for Light and another for Dark.

It allows you to define plenty of styling options for each widget and is much easier to use than CSS.

 

  • To achieve the Light theme, the Background color for each widget should be Light grey color, select light theme as DEFAULT.
  • For the dark theme, the background color for each widget should be light black.

Step 3:

  • Create a switch component by Insert -> Filters/Controls -> Switch

  • Set switch value data source type as "Script variables"

  • Provide the ID Name for a switch component as "Switch_2"

Step 4:

  • Once done with theme preferences, Now it is time to Script the logic
  • It is necessary to script logic in switch's fx onChange() and story's onInitalization()

  • Now in switch's fx onChange() , The script toggles between light and dark themes based on the current ThemeMode.

    1. If the theme is dark:

      • Switch to the light theme by using (Application.setTheme()). 
      • Application.setTheme()Click the Ctrl+space bar. It will allow you to choose the corresponding theme preferences automatically and fetch the theme ID.

theme selection

  • I used Icons for both light and dark theme, Initially Hide DarkIcon and show LightIcon.

  • The script toggles between light and dark themes based on the current ThemeMode.

    1. If the theme is dark:

      • Switch to light theme (Application.setTheme()).
      • Hide DarkIcon and show LightIcon.

    2. Otherwise (theme is light):

      • Switch to the dark theme.
      • Hide LightIcon and show DarkIcon.

    This ensures dynamic theme and icon adjustments based on user interaction.

  • Code:
  • if ( ThemeMode === "dark" )
    {
                    ThemeMode = "light";
                    Application.setTheme("369CEA6B9DC57C13BF5FA65BB646871C");
    DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
    LightIcon.setVisible(true);
     
    }
    else{
    Application.setTheme("DE00DBCBBDA02C61A64EF48759CF3E43");
                    ThemeMode = "dark";
    LightIcon.setVisible(false);
    DarkIcon.setVisible(true);
     
     
                    }
     

  • Now in the Story's fx, select onInitialization() and The script initializes LightIcon as visible and DarkIcon as hidden. It then toggles their visibility based on the ON/OFF state of Switch_2.

  • Code
DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
LightIcon.setVisible(true);
 
 
if( Switch_2.isOn() === true){
DarkIcon.setVisible(true);
LightIcon.setVisible(false);
} else{
LightIcon.setVisible(true);  
DarkIcon.setVisible(false);
}

Don't forget to Save the story and view it!

Hurraayyyy successfully implemented the Light and Dark theme in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) 😎.

entry.title: Re: Brownfield Conversion from EHS Product Safety (ECC) t...
entry.content:
Hello

your list is in my opinion incomplete

Hence, we identified the following master data. If this list is not complete according to your experience, then any remarks are highly appreciated and thank you in advance 

  • CG02 specification (realsub and so on)
  • Material Master
  • WWI Template 
  • generated SDS raw WWI reports
  • SDS shipment history (CVD1)
  • Customer master data (SDS receiver)

I am missing e.g. "phrases" and for a SDS you need chater 14 and therefore you need "DG" set up. (could be that you are referring to "realsub and so on): but DG data is important

Never have seen a company using EHS only for report shipment..

MOst of the companies are using it with "DG", "SVT" and "GLM". Only then you have benefit

As explained: the "trick" is to define the scope (and aprahc of data migration) of the project carefully

So we have "e.g. greenfield". Using greenfield I would only migrate the "needed data"; ideally: data cleansing is done before (ore afterwards)

But the "exact" approach depends: is the company still using "3e" content; are they planing to use "3E" content?

and many other topics

from practical point of view: the IT process will work; but the business do not understand the "project" (e.g. data validation is not done or not done carefully by the customer).

At the end: the customer can then have "mess" in the system. But old system is still "shut down". So they have to use the new system. in 80% of the cases i know: a 1 uptto 2 year "data cleansing" project must be started after the migration as the migration was not done carefully.

The "most" primitive version is: target system has same set up as source system; no data cleansing done.

But as this type of projcets is a "high invest": i propose to prepare good data migration path.

Any how: i wish you success

C.B.

PS i have not listed here an "IT tool" for migration. The reason is: only you can define the "best" tool/approach; the list of variants: how to do the job.. is simply to long to discuss this here

entry.title: How to Reset a Cloud Foundry Droplet to a Previous Version and download the droplet code
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of software development, rolling out updates is a routine task. However, not every update goes as planned. Sometimes, a newly deployed version of an application introduces unforeseen issues, and rolling back to a previous stable version becomes essential. Cloud Foundry (CF) makes this process straightforward with its built-in support for managing application droplets. In this post, we’ll walk through the steps to reset a CF droplet to a previous version and download a droplet.

What is a Droplet in Cloud Foundry?
A droplet is an immutable snapshot of your application code, its dependencies, and the runtime environment created during the staging process. When you deploy an app to CF, it stages the app, generates a droplet, and assigns it to the app’s instances. Each droplet represents a specific version of your application. CF keeps up to 5 previous droplets in a staged state. Older droplets are marked as expired.

Why Reset a Droplet?
You might want to reset a droplet to a previous version for various reasons:

  • The new version of the application has critical bugs or performance issues.
  • A misconfiguration was introduced in the latest deployment.
  • There’s a need to quickly restore service stability.

Prerequisites
Before resetting or downloading a droplet, ensure the following:

  1. Install cf cli if it is not already installed. You can download it from the official cf site.
  2. Ensure you have access to the Cloud Foundry CLI (cf command-line tool).
  3. Make sure you have the necessary permissions to view application history and make updates.
  4. Verify that the previous droplets are available and listed in the app’s droplet history.
  5. Install the multiapps plugin, if not already installed, using the following command:

 

cf install-plugin multiapps

 

 

Step-by-Step Guide to Reset a Droplet

1. Login to CF
Login to CF using the following command

 

cf login -u <userId> -a <API_URL>

 

Or use Single Sign-On (SSO):

 

cf login -u <userId> -a <API_URL> --sso

 

Replace <userId> and <API_URL> with your user ID and the public API endpoint

2. List Available Droplets
Retrieve the list of available droplets for your application:

 

cf droplets <app-name>

 

This command will display all droplets associated with the application, including their GUID, creation date, and state. Identify the GUID of the droplet you want to reset.

3. Change the Current Droplet
Change the current droplet to a previous one using the following command:

 

cf set-droplet <app-name> <guid-droplet>

 

Replace <guid-droplet> with the GUID of the desired droplet.

4.Restart the App
After changing the droplet, restart the application:

 

cf restart <app-name>

 

This step is mandatory to apply the changes. After restarting, verify the current droplet with:

 

cf droplets <app-name>

 

 

Downloading a Droplet

Downloading a droplet allows you to retrieve a specific version of your application for debugging, analysis, or redeployment.

1. List Available Droplets
Retrieve the list of available droplets for your application:

 

cf droplets <app-name>

 

Identify the GUID of the droplet you want to download.

2. Download the Droplet
Use the cf curl command to download the droplet:

 

cf curl /v3/droplets/<droplet-guid>/download --output <filename>

 

Replace <droplet-guid> with the GUID of the desired droplet.
Replace <filename> with the desired file name for saving the droplet locally (e.g., my-droplet.tar).

Example:

 

cf droplets my-app cf curl /v3/droplets/abc12345-6789-def0-ghij-klmnopqrstuv/download --output my-app-droplet.tar

 

The downloaded file will be a tarball containing the application code, dependencies, and runtime environment.

Conclusion
Resetting a droplet in Cloud Foundry is a lifesaver when you need to revert to a stable version of your application quickly. Additionally, downloading droplets and examining can provide deeper insights into your application’s state for debugging or analysis. 

Have you faced challenges with rolling back or downloading droplets in Cloud Foundry? Share your experiences and solutions!

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors: How to Create and Run Story Reports
entry.content:
Story Reporting in SAP SuccessFactors is a powerful tool that allows users to easily create, customize, and analyze reports. Story Reports in SAP SuccessFactors provide a modern, user-friendly approach to creating detailed and visually engaging reports. Unlike traditional reporting tools, they blend powerful data analysis with interactive visualizations, making it easier to understand and share insights effectively.

Story is a presentation-style report that uses visualizations (chart, image, text, table, shape) to describe data. Story reports consist of pages. Pages are made up of:

  • Visualizations of Data
  • Filters and Input Controls to manipulate the data

Stories use data sources (queries) that retrieve your desired reporting and use widgets to represent the data. To build or update queries, you work in the query designer. You work on the story canvas to manage the representation of the data the queries retrieve.

Query Designer

The Query Designer enables you to select the fields you want to use to create reports using Story. The queries you build using the Query Designer are stored within the Stories and cannot be accessed outside the Story. They are saved as a data source(s) for the Story.

Why Use Story Reports?

  1. Integrated Experience: Story Reports integrate seamlessly with SAP SuccessFactors modules, allowing users to pull data from various sources into one cohesive report.
  2. Interactive Visualizations: Unlike other reporting tools, Story Reports provides dynamic charts and graphs, enabling users to interact with data and uncover insights quickly.
  3. Customization: Users can customize their reports, tailoring them to specific business needs with easy drag-and-drop functionality.
  4. Real-Time Data: Story Reports offer real-time data, which ensures that users always work with the latest information for timely decision-making.
  5. User-Friendly Interface: Designed with business users in mind, Story Reports is easier to use than some of the more technical reporting tools like Advanced Reporting. 

 

Create a Story Report

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Please refer to the high-level steps to create a story report:

  • Navigate to Report Center.
  • Choose New and select Story.

 

  • Select a Design Mode Type (Optimized or Classic).

  • Build a query in Query Designer.

 

 

 

 

a. Browse the module data sets in the Available Data section, and expand the data set for your report.

b. To select a table for your query, double-click it or drag and drop it on the query canvas.

c. On the query canvas, select the table where you want to build your query.

 

 

 

 

d. Add columns and filters to build your query, using the options on the action menu.

 

 

 

 

 

e. When you have completed your query, choose Finish.

f. Enter a data source name.

 

 

 

 

g. Choose OK to save the query.

  • Add and configure widgets in your story in Story Canvas.

 

 

 

 

Add charts and tables to represent your data.

 

 

 

 

Add filters and input controls to help analyze the data.

Add text, images, and shapes to support the display and understanding of the report.

 

 

 

  • Save the Report using the File Menu.

These steps help streamline the process of creating and running reports, making it easier to analyze and present your data effectively.

Story Reporting in SAP SuccessFactors is a powerful yet user-friendly tool that allows you to transform raw data into meaningful insights. By following the simple steps outlined in this guide, you can create and run reports that not only meet your business needs but also enhance data-driven decision-making across the business.

As part of SAP's vision to enhance reporting capabilities in People Analytics, SAP is transitioning to Stories in People Analytics as the primary reporting solution. This shift will streamline and modernize reporting processes, making it easier for users to create insightful and visually compelling reports.

To facilitate this transition, SAP has announced the deprecation of legacy tools, including Table reports, Canvas reports, and Tiles & Dashboards. After November 21, 2025, these legacy tools will no longer be available. Businesses are encouraged to transition to Story Reporting to continue their reporting operations without disruption.

If you are planning the transition to Story Reporting, please refer to the implementation steps here

I hope this blog has given a clear and practical understanding of how to create and run Story Reports in SAP SuccessFactors. As you gain more experience, you will discover even more ways to customize and optimize the story reports. Stay curious and keep exploring the possibilities to make the most out of your reporting capabilities.

Happy reporting!

Please feel free to share your experiences and learning 🙂If you have any questions or suggestions, please add a comment below. Thank you 🙂

entry.title: Dependency Formula for BOM qty
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I am currently supporting in a public cloud project and one of my colleagues has the following problem: 

For a finished product, we have a bill of materials (BOM) where we want to control the quantity of a component based on the quantity of the finished product. For example, if the order quantity is between 1-80 units, the quantity of the component should be 5; if it's between 81-160 units, the quantity should be 10; if it's between 161-240 units, the quantity should be 15, and so on. How should we proceed? I have already read other posts pointing to variant configuration and object dependency, but I have no prior experience with these topics and feel a bit lost.

 

Thankful for any help

entry.title: Re: Dependency Formula for BOM qty
entry.content:
1. Understand the Basics of Variant Configuration

Variant configuration helps in managing BOMs and rules for products with variations. Key concepts include:

  • Characteristics: Define attributes of the product (e.g., Order Quantity).
  • Class: Group characteristics into a classification system.

Dependencies: Define rules using formulas to control BOM components or operations

  1. Create the Necessary Configuration Objects
  2. a) Create a Characteristic for Order Quantity
  1. Go to transaction CT04 (or equivalent in your system).
  2. Create a new characteristic (e.g., ORDER_QUANTITY).
  3. Assign data type Numeric.
  4. Set Value Range: [1, 9999].
  1. b) Define a Class
  1. Go to transaction CL02.
  2. Create a class (e.g., PRODUCT_CLASS) of type 300 (variant configuration).
  3. Assign the characteristic ORDER_QUANTITY to this class.
  1. c) Maintain BOM
  1. Go to transaction CS01 to create a BOM for the finished product.
  2. For the component whose quantity changes based on order size, set the quantity field as a formula field

entry.title: Re: ass0ciate consultant
entry.content:
1. Enterprise Structure Configuration

1.1 Define Company Code

  • T-Code: OX02
    • Company Name
    • City
    • Country
    • Currency
    • Address
    • Go to OX02 and click New Entries.
    • Define a unique company code and fill in details like:

1.2 Define Plant

  • T-Code: OX10
    • Define plants that represent manufacturing, production, or storage locations.
    • Click New Entries, specify a plant ID, and maintain the necessary details such as name, address, and region.

1.3 Define Storage Location

  • T-Code: OMJJ
    • Assign storage locations under plants for inventory tracking.
    • Specify storage location codes and maintain their descriptions.

1.4 Assign Plant to Company Code

  • T-Code: OX17
    • Map each plant to its company code.

1.5 Assign Purchasing Organization to Plant/Company Code

  • T-Code: OX18
    • Define and assign purchasing organizations to plants for procurement processes.
  1. Material Master Configuration

2.1 Define Material Types

  • T-Code: OMSR
    • Finished Goods (FERT)
    • Raw Materials (ROH)
    • Semi-Finished Products (HALB)
    • Material types control material grouping and behavior.
    • Examples include:

2.2 Define Number Ranges for Materials

  • T-Code: MMNR
    • Set up internal and external number ranges for each material type.

2.3 Maintain Field Selection for Data Screens

  • T-Code: OMS9
    • Define which fields (e.g., size, weight) are required, optional, or hidden during material master creation.
  1. Vendor Master Configuration

3.1 Create Vendor Account Groups

  • T-Code: OBD3
    • Local Vendors
    • Overseas Vendors
    • Vendor account groups control vendor classifications.
    • Examples:

3.2 Define Number Ranges for Vendors

  • T-Code: XKN1
    • Assign internal or external number ranges to vendor account groups.

3.3 Assign Vendor Number Ranges to Account Groups

  • T-Code: OBAS
  1. Purchasing Configuration

4.1 Define Purchasing Groups

  • T-Code: OME4
    • Purchasing groups represent buyers responsible for procurement.

4.2 Define Document Types for Purchase Orders

  • T-Code: OMEB
    • Configure purchase order (PO) types (e.g., standard PO, stock transfer PO).

4.3 Define Release Procedures for Purchase Documents

  • T-Code: OMGQ
    • Set up release (approval) strategies for purchase requisitions and purchase orders based on criteria like value and plant.
  1. Inventory Management Configuration

5.1 Define Movement Types

  • T-Code: OMWN
    • Goods Receipt (101)
    • Goods Issue (201)
    • Movement types dictate how inventory transactions occur.
    • Examples:

5.2 Maintain Plant Parameters

  • T-Code: OMJ3
    • Define inventory parameters specific to plants (e.g., batch management settings).
  1. Invoice Verification Configuration

6.1 Define Tolerance Limits

  • T-Code: OMR6
    • Set up tolerance limits for invoice amounts and quantity deviations.

6.2 Configure Automatic Postings

  • T-Code: OBYC
    • Map inventory transactions to general ledger (GL) accounts for automatic postings.
  1. Additional Configuration Steps

7.1 Material Valuation

  • T-Code: OMWM
    • Configure material valuation methods (e.g., moving average, standard price).

7.2 Configure Pricing Procedures

  • T-Code: M/08
    • Set up pricing schemas and conditions for vendor quotations and purchasing documents.

entry.title: Issues with (Sign out user (Firebase Auth)) and Suggestions for Firebase Integration Improvements
entry.content:
Issue Description

Hello, SAP Build Apps team and community,

I’ve been experiencing some challenges with the (Sign out user (Firebase Auth)) component and would like to report the issues while also suggesting a few functionalities that could improve Firebase integration in SAP Build Apps.

  1. Problem with navigation after Firebase Auth Sign Out:
    • When the (Sign out user (Firebase Auth)) component is triggered, the user is successfully signed out (confirmed on the Firebase backend). However, the app does not automatically navigate to the login page as expected.
    • On the web preview, navigation to the login page works as intended, but on Android, after the sign-out, the navigation component does not redirect to the desired page.
    • This forced us to improvise by redirecting the user to a blocking page instead of the login page.

Question: Is there any official configuration or workaround to resolve this issue on Android? If not, could this functionality be revisited in future updates?

Suggestions for Improvements

  1. Enable Firebase SDK on the client-side (JavaScript):
    • While we understand SAP Build Apps already has Firebase integration, allowing developers to use the Firebase SDK directly on the client-side (via JavaScript) would open up many possibilities:
      • Implementing custom authentication logic.
      • Accessing advanced functionalities such as onAuthStateChanged or dynamic Firestore rules.
      • Simplifying the use of libraries like firebase-admin or firebase/auth to address specific issues not currently supported by the native integration.
    • Since SAP Build Apps already supports JavaScript in other areas, integrating the Firebase SDK directly may not be overly complex.

Question: Are there any plans to enable Firebase SDK support on the client side? If not, could this functionality be considered for the roadmap?

  1. Expand support for Firebase Auth persistence on mobile devices:
    • Currently, the (Set auth state persistence (Firebase Auth)) component only works on the web. On mobile devices (Android/iOS), it’s not possible to manage authentication state persistence.
    • This limitation complicates scenarios where we need to log out users or validate sessions dynamically.

Question: Is there any possibility of adding support for Firebase Auth state persistence on mobile devices?

Summary of Suggestions:

  • Review the behavior of the (Sign out user (Firebase Auth)) component on Android to ensure proper post-logout navigation.
  • Consider enabling Firebase SDK support on the client side, allowing for greater flexibility.
  • Add support for Firebase Auth state persistence on mobile platforms.

I would like to know if other developers have encountered these issues and if the SAP Build Apps team has any suggestions for overcoming these challenges or plans to implement these functionalities in the future.

Thank you for your attention and for the excellent work on the platform!

entry.title: Execute immediate returning a table as output
entry.content:
the following code does not compile

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE schema.GET_MISSING_ROWS(
IN source_table NVARCHAR(50),
IN target_table NVARCHAR(50),
IN select_clause NVARCHAR(50),
IN where_clause NVARCHAR(50),
OUT result_rows TABLE (EMPLID VARCHAR(11), EMPL_RCD INTEGER, EFFDT DATE, EFFSEQ INTEGER, GVT_SF50_REMARK VARCHAR(3))
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
AS
BEGIN
-- Construct and execute dynamic SQL to find missing rows
DECLARE sql_stmt NVARCHAR(5000);

sql_stmt := 'SELECT ' + :select_clause + ' FROM ' + :source_table + ' src ' + 'WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ' || :target_table || ' tgt WHERE ' || :where_clause || ')';
result_rows := EXECUTE IMMEDIATE (:sql_stmt);
END;

error:

Could not execute 'CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE schema.GET_MISSING_ROWS( IN source_table NVARCHAR(50), IN ...'
SAP DBTech JDBC: [257]: sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near "IMMEDIATE": line 16 col 28 (at pos 672)

entry.title: SAP A.I. Privacy and Security
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

An important factor with generative A.I. is security - Does our data we submit to an A.I. train a model and potentially thereby expose confidential information?

AWS Bedrock is an A.I. service offered by Amazon and they clearly state that it is secure and private. It is safe to submit confidential information.

SAP A.I. Launchpad / Generative A.I. Hub offers access to many models - are these models hosted on SAP servers or does it just connect to the A.I. providers servers?

We're looking for a definitive answer that submitting confidential/sensitive data to a model on SAP A.I. Launchpad is safe, and that our data is not exposed to the public internet or used to train models?

Kind Regards,

Reinhardt

entry.title: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content:
I just asked a publicly GPT trained model at my company, to generate the documentation of an old and huge ABAP program, to see if ChatGPT can save me some time to understand what the program does.

The prompt is limited to one or two thousand characters, so I can only ask for one portion of a subroutine at a time. The generated text is just good for explaining the meaning of each individual ABAP statement. It cannot save time.

Below are two questions and answers for your information.

Did you have a better experience with GenAI for generating a good ABAP documentation?

Which engine do you use? (public, SAP Joule, privately trained model, etc.)

Thank you.

Sandra

First prompt:

Describe the detailed steps of the subroutine SAVE_COMMENT. Here is its code: FORM save_comment USING p_vl_max_fake p_ok_boolean. CLEAR sg_zfake_user. MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_2. IF gw_mode = 'CREER'. MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_1. MOVE 'E' TO sg_zfake_user-statut. ELSE. DELETE FROM zfake_user WHERE nfake = gi_nfake AND typefake = '2'. IF sy-subrc <> 0. ENDIF. MOVE g_date_1 TO sg_zfake_user-date_1. MOVE g_statut TO sg_zfake_user-statut. ENDIF. MOVE gi_nfake TO sg_zfake_user-nfake. MOVE '2' TO sg_zfake_user-typefake. MOVE 0 TO sg_zfake_user-notypefake. PERFORM affichage_commentaire USING 'GET' tg_fake_commentaire_user_m tg_fake_commentaire_admin_m. LOOP AT tg_fake_commentaire_user_m INTO sg_fake_commentaire. CONCATENATE sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 sg_fake_commentaire INTO sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 SEPARATED BY space. ENDLOOP. CONDENSE sg_zfake_user-rubrique1. MOVE sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 TO g_rubrique1. LOOP AT tg_fake_commentaire_admin_m INTO sg_fake_commentaire. CONCATENATE sg_zfake_user-rubrique2 sg_fake_commentaire INTO sg_zfake_user-rubrique2 SEPARATED BY space. ENDLOOP. CONDENSE sg_zfake_user-rubrique2. MOVE sg_zfake_user-rubrique2 TO g_rubrique2. MOVE p_encopiede TO sg_zfake_user-rubrique3. IF p_encopiede IS NOT INITIAL. CLEAR : b_mdp_ecc, b_sup_ecc , b_sup_decisionnel, sg_zfake_user-rubrique4, sg_zfake_user-rubrique6, sg_zfake_user-rubrique7. ELSE. IF b_mdp_ecc = 'X'. MOVE 'X' TO sg_zfake_user-rubrique4. ENDIF. IF b_mdp_infocentre = 'X'. MOVE 'X' TO sg_zfake_user-rubrique5. ENDIF. IF b_sup_ecc = 'X'. MOVE 'X' TO sg_zfake_user-rubrique6. ENDIF. IF b_sup_decisionnel = 'X'. MOVE 'X' TO sg_zfake_user-rubrique7. ENDIF. ENDIF. INSERT zfake_user FROM sg_zfake_user. IF sy-subrc <> 0. UPDATE zfake_user FROM sg_zfake_user. ENDIF. IF sy-subrc <> 0. MESSAGE 'Cannot save the comments' TYPE 'S' p_ok_boolean = 'X'. EXIT. ENDIF. ENDFORM.

The answer:

There’s a problem processing your request. Please try again later or let me know if I can help.

I shortened the prompt:

Describe the detailed steps of the subroutine SAVE_COMMENT. Here is its code: FORM save_comment USING p_vl_max_fake p_ok_boolean. CLEAR sg_zfake_user. MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_2. IF gw_mode = 'CREER'. MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_1. MOVE 'E' TO sg_zfake_user-statut. ELSE. DELETE FROM zfake_user WHERE nfake = gi_nfake AND typefake = '2'. IF sy-subrc <> 0. ENDIF. MOVE g_date_1 TO sg_zfake_user-date_1. MOVE g_statut TO sg_zfake_user-statut. ENDIF. MOVE gi_nfake TO sg_zfake_user-nfake. MOVE '2' TO sg_zfake_user-typefake. MOVE 0 TO sg_zfake_user-notypefake. PERFORM affichage_commentaire USING 'GET' tg_fake_commentaire_user_m tg_fake_commentaire_admin_m. LOOP AT tg_fake_commentaire_user_m INTO sg_fake_commentaire. CONCATENATE sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 sg_fake_commentaire INTO sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 SEPARATED BY space. ENDLOOP.

The answer:

Using publicly trained GPT model...

Sure, let's break down the subroutine `SAVE_COMMENT` step by step:

Subroutine Definition
FORM save_comment USING p_vl_max_fake p_ok_boolean.
The subroutine SAVE_COMMENT is defined with two parameters: p_vl_max_fake and p_ok_boolean.

Step 1: Clear the Work Area
CLEAR sg_zfake_user.
The work area sg_zfake_user is cleared to ensure it starts with no data.

Step 2: Set the Current Date
MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_2.
The current system date (sy-datum) is moved to the field date_2 of the work area sg_zfake_user.

Step 3: Check the Mode
IF gw_mode = 'CREER'.
The subroutine checks if the mode (gw_mode) is 'CREER' (which likely means 'create').

Step 3.1: If Mode is 'CREER'
MOVE sy-datum TO sg_zfake_user-date_1.
MOVE 'E' TO sg_zfake_user-statut.
The current system date (sy-datum) is moved to the field date_1 of the work area sg_zfake_user.
The status field statut of the work area sg_zfake_user is set to 'E'.

Step 3.2: If Mode is Not 'CREER'
ELSE.
DELETE FROM zfake_user WHERE nfake = gi_nfake AND typefake = '2'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
ENDIF.
MOVE g_date_1 TO sg_zfake_user-date_1.
MOVE g_statut TO sg_zfake_user-statut.
The subroutine deletes entries from the database table zfake_user where nfake equals gi_nfake and typefake equals '2'.
If the deletion is not successful (sy-subrc is not zero), it does nothing (empty ENDIF).
The value of g_date_1 is moved to the field date_1 of the work area sg_zfake_user.
The value of g_statut is moved to the field statut of the work area sg_zfake_user.

Step 4: Set Additional Fields
MOVE gi_nfake TO sg_zfake_user-nfake.
MOVE '2' TO sg_zfake_user-typefake.
MOVE 0 TO sg_zfake_user-notypefake.
The value of gi_nfake is moved to the field nfake of the work area sg_zfake_user.
The value '2' is moved to the field typefake of the work area sg_zfake_user.
The value 0 is moved to the field notypefake of the work area sg_zfake_user.

Step 5: Perform Another Subroutine
PERFORM affichage_commentaire USING 'GET' tg_fake_commentaire_user_m tg_fake_commentaire_admin_m.
The subroutine affichage_commentaire is called with the parameters 'GET', tg_fake_commentaire_user_m, and tg_fake_commentaire_admin_m.

Step 6: Loop Through Internal Table
LOOP AT tg_fake_commentaire_user_m INTO sg_fake_commentaire.
CONCATENATE sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 sg_fake_commentaire INTO sg_zfake_user-rubrique1 SEPARATED BY space.
ENDLOOP.
The subroutine loops through the internal table tg_fake_commentaire_user_m and reads each entry into the work area sg_fake_commentaire.
For each entry, it concatenates the value of sg_fake_commentaire to the field rubrique1 of the work area sg_zfake_user, separated by a space.

End of Subroutine
ENDFORM.
The subroutine ends.

Summary
The SAVE_COMMENT subroutine performs the following actions:

  • Clears the work area sg_zfake_user.
  • Sets the current date in date_2.
  • Depending on the mode (gw_mode), it either sets the creation date and status or deletes existing entries and sets the date and status from global variables.
  • Sets additional fields in the work area.
  • Calls another subroutine affichage_commentaire.
  • Loops through an internal table and concatenates its entries to a field in the work area.

entry.title: Re: Generative AI for SAP. Part V: Models and Knowledge Graphs (KG)
entry.content:
Hi Mario,

I don't agree that using LLM models is the appropriate way to build Knowledge Graphs with SAP data. While LLMs are adequate in building Knowledge Graphs from unstructured data, this approach is not optimal or necessary in an SAP environment. Here's why:

  1. SAP systems already provide
    • Structured data with well-defined schemas
    • Clear entity relationships through the data model
    • Built-in metadata about tables, fields and relationships
    • Validated and cleansed data
  2. The appropriate approach for SAP data would be to
    • Extract metadata from the SAP data model (CDS tables, APIs,...)
    • Use this metadata to define the ontology (SAP has presented a tool in BTP in the last teched 2024 that will help to do this but you don't have to wait...)
    • Transform the existing structured data (each customer with its data) into RDF triples.
    • Convert the SAP data model into semantic relationships.
    • Store these triples in a Knowledge Graph database (bearing in mind that HANA Graph does not currently support this, but has announced that a KG engine will be released on HANA).

Also, I noticed that your post mixes concepts between Labeled Property Graphs (LPG) and Knowledge Graphs. The automotive model you present is clearly an LPG, not a Knowledge Graph, as it has these key LPG features:

  1. Node labels and properties:
    • Each node (vehicle, manufacturer, customer, etc.) has a clear label.
    • Nodes have well-defined properties/attributes (e.g. vehicle has VIN, make, model, etc.).
  2. Edge labels and directionality:
    • Relationships between nodes are explicitly labelled and directional (e.g., the vehicle is ‘made by’ the manufacturer).
    • The cardinality of relationships is clearly specified (one-to-many, many-to-many, etc.).
  3. Property-centred structure:
    • The model focuses on operational properties and business relationships.
    • Relationships are structural rather than semantic.

In contrast, knowledge graphs typically

  • Include ontologies and semantic relations
  • Focus on conceptual relationships and reasoning.
  • Use RDF (subject-predicate-object) triples.
  • Emphasise inference capabilities.
  • Include class hierarchies and inheritance.

I agree with your broader view on combining LLMs with knowledge representation (KR). In particular, in my view, through Autonomous Agents. LLMs have finally made flexible and open conversational interfaces possible, while curated knowledge graphs can provide reliable and auditable sources of information to help prevent hallucinations. However, building effective autonomous agents requires three key components:

  1. Knowledge representation and reasoning
  2. Planning
  3. Conversational ability

Although LLMs are good at conversation (#3), they do not inherently provide KR or planning capabilities (#1 and #2). A trivially simplified workflow could be as follows

  1. The human user provides a task to the agent using natural language.
  2. Agent interprets the task (LLM)
  3. Task is translated into formal goals (KR, potentially assisted by LLM)
  4. Agent creates an execution plan (planning, KR)
  5. Plan is executed
  6. Results are translated for human consumption (LLM)

The future is not simply about using LLM to build ontologies and knowledge graphs, nor is it about using knowledge graphs to enhance RAG techniques. Rather, it is about carefully combining these technologies to leverage their respective strengths while mitigating their weaknesses.

AND "[delta_field]" >= ( SELECT "DATE" FROM "[DELTA Table]" WHERE "EXTRACTOR" = '[extractor_name]' )

entry.title: CAP Project - SQL query response limitation 1000 records
entry.content:
Within our CAP project we have defined entities that we have created services for. To extract more than 1000 records we have defined the annotation which works well for a service.

 

service CatalogService @(path: '/srv') {

@cds.query.limit.max: 1500
entity AnaToBeDraftInterBill as projection on lng.TOBEDRAFT_INTERBILL9;

 

However, we have a function defined inside the CAP project that has SQL that directly reads our entity in the HANA Db and this query is getting restricted by 1000 entries. How can we overcome this restriction? It seems odd to me as within the HANA DB Explorer I can perform the same SQL statement in the console and return all the records.

 

 SELECT`project_number`.from`project_numbers`
  .where`sales_office = ${office}`.orderBy`project_number asc`

 

Thanks

entry.title: Asset Manager - unable to open login page after MDK updated
entry.content:
I updated extensions and MDK to the newest versions. Now when users want to start onboarding, the login page does not open. Users open the application, aggress to EULA, press Get Started, scan a QR code, confirm the URL, a loading indicator appears and the previous screen with the Get Started buttons opens again.

Previous version:

  • SAM Metadata: 2310
  • SAM Extensions: 2310.0.3
  • MDK: 23.8.7

Current version:

  • SAM Metadata: 2310
  • SAM Extensions: 2410.0.2
  • MDK: 24.7.6

I am sure I am using the right SapCloudPlatformEndpoint URL. The problem is the same when using configuration from BrandedSettings.json or QR code.

Configuration in BTP should be correct. When I try an older build before MDK + extensions update, onboarding works fine.

On a virtualized Android device behavior is a little different. When the login page should open, the error "HTTP Status 400 - " occurs. The web page is opened in Asset Manager in some built-in Chrome window. But the result is the same (Oauth failed).

Edit: I just downloaded all the files for SSAM 2410.0.2 (metadata, SAM.mdkproject, etc), built it with MDK 4.7.6 , and uploaded it to the device with VSC sap-mdk 1.27.0. Instead of the login page "I got HTTP Status 400 -".

entry.title: Re: Webhooks in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Hi @Saurabh_Kabra 

Calls from autoflow are Async and not creating a overhead on client side. Indeed if the autoflow condition is matched a call is made and generates traffic on your service side. 

 

For Sync Webhooks the setup is different. Your are configuring "External Hooks" in the Extensibility Administration. If you have a look in the code on this webpage, it is responding sync. The setup is shown in the video.

entry.title: Re: Extensibility Overview SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
Web Component Mashups can be enabled on your tenant. Please check https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SALES/348d3cace0eb4146a4af6e018cbbb88c/dd49b24d4f144375b3e92f7454b84df1.html?parentHref=%252Fwhats-new%252F58f0cc34e3c141a793ba8dfb7fc47f21%253FArea%253DExtensibility%2526locale%253Den-US&parentName=What%27s%2520New%2520in%2520SAP%2520Sales%2520Cloud%2520Version%25C2%25A02 for details. 

Technically Web Components are rendered as iFrames too to enforce a certain level of security and separation between V2 and the extension. So at the end I am not sure, if this feature is giving any benefit over classic iFrames except of slightly simpler code. 

So would be interesting your thoughts on the topic.

entry.title: How to define the "data-sap-ui-resource-roots" of OPA qunit test for MTA project (Fiori freestyle)
entry.content:
Hi,

We are building a testing modules for existing full stack SAP UI5 project, our project structure looks like this..

project
-app
--node_modules
--webapp
---myAppInst
----controller
----views
----resources(my question is here)
----test
------Test.qunit.js
----manifest
---xs-app.json
---package.json
-db
-srv
-xs-security.json
-node_modules
-package.json

i found SAPUI5 and OPENUI5 is using different resources folder, like the sap/ui/test modules,  when i tried to install the modules, files were created in my node_modules folder, but when i running the Test.qunit.html, it is always referring to the resources folder. Therefore, to make it work, i copied the openUI module file from node_modules to /webapp/myAppInst/resource folder. 

here is my Test.qunit.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
	
	<!-- src="../../../../node_modules/@openui5/sap.ui.core/src/sap/ui/test/starter/runTest.js" -->
	<meta charset="utf-8">
	<script
	src="../resources/sap/ui/test/starter/runTest.js"
		data-sap-ui-resource-roots='{
			"test-resources.myAppInst": "./"
		}'
	></script>
</head>
<body class="sapUiBody">
	<div id="qunit"></div>
	<div id="qunit-fixture"></div>
</body>
